name: Kung Pao Chicken
recipeIngredient: 3 chicken breast halves, skinned and boned
6 small dried hot chili peppers, or to taste
2 green onions with tops, cut into 1 1/2-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. For the marinade, combine 2 Tbsp cornstarch, 2 Tbsp soy sauce, 2 Tbsp sherry, and salt in a large bowl; mix well. Add chicken and stir to coat thoroughly. Let stand for 30 minutes.
2. Combine remaining 1 1/2 Tbsp cornstarch, 3 Tbsp soy sauce, 3 Tbsp sherry, vinegar, chicken broth or water, and sugar in a small bowl; mix well. Set aside.
3. Heat 1 Tbsp oil in a wok or large skillet over medium heat. Add peanuts; cook and stir until lightly toasted. Remove peanuts from the wok; set aside.
4. Increase heat to high. Add chili peppers; stir-fry until they just begin to char, about 1 minute.
5. Add chicken mixture; stir-fry for 2 minutes. Add ginger; stir-fry until chicken is no longer pink in the center, about 1 minute.
6. Add peanuts and onions; stir fry for 1 minute.
7. Stir cornstarch mixture; add to wok. Cook and stir until the sauce boils and thickens. Garnish with green onion and dried hot chili peppers, if desired.
8. Makes 3 servings.
